response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement — which the candidate does not possess (his listed languages are English, Romanian, and German). Beyond the language barrier, the role demands deep C programming for embedded firmware, Linux kernel source-level expertise, and hands-on security/networking knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching), none of which are evidenced in his resume. His brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in a thesis project does not constitute the required proficiency in an embedded security context.
strengths: Long and consistent career demonstrating broad software engineering competency across multiple languages and platforms
Some embedded systems exposure at Altom Consulting (robotic arms, hardware control) and low-level C/C++ work in academic/personal projects
Strong Linux usage and DevOps/infrastructure experience (Docker, CI, GitLab, Kubernetes) that could support embedded development environments
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement that immediately disqualifies the candidate
No demonstrable experience in network security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption) or L2/L3 switch networking
Primary tech stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web; C programming depth is superficial and not in an embedded firmware or UNIX systems context

key_factors.factor: No network security or switch knowledge
